era: Portland/Seattle, Oregon/Washington. Indie Experimental. E=4 -- post-corridor moderation. Kill Rock Stars label. Chamber folk meets experimental arrangements.

location.formative: The Portland-Seattle corridor. Portland's literary folk tradition meets Olympia's independent label infrastructure. Kill Rock Stars (Olympia, WA). I-5 as cultural artery.
location.career: Horse Feathers -- chamber folk/experimental. Justin Ringle. Kill Rock Stars. Portland to Seattle corridor. E=4 -- the chamber quiet.

members.name: Justin Ringle
members.note: The creative lead. Literary lyrics, fingerpicked acoustic guitar, hushed vocals. The project is Ringle's vision realized through rotating collaborators.

associated_musicians.name: Bikini Kill
associated_musicians.note: Riot grrrl originators. Kathleen Hanna. Olympia. KRS roster.

associated_musicians.name: Sleater-Kinney
associated_musicians.note: Brownstein, Tucker, Weiss. Portland/Olympia. KRS's most prominent act.

associated_musicians.name: Elliott Smith
associated_musicians.note: Singer-songwriter. Portland. Early material and compilations on KRS.

associated_musicians.name: Unwound
associated_musicians.note: Olympia post-hardcore. One of KRS's earliest signings.

associated_musicians.name: The Decemberists
associated_musicians.note: Colin Meloy's literary indie rock. Portland. Early releases on KRS.

associated_musicians.name: Damien Jurado
associated_musicians.note: Quiet, introspective singer-songwriter. Independent label. Literary folk PNW tradition.

theory_nodes.concept: The chamber quiet -- acoustic element: acoustic guitar foundation plus chamber instrumentation (violin, banjo, cello) plus experimental textures; intimate and orchestral simultaneously; E=4 as the balance between whisper-quiet and dynamic chamber intensity
examples: Horse Feathers (degree 124) -- chamber folk/experimental. E=4. Fingerpicked guitar + violin + banjo + cello + tape loops.
Thistled Spring (2008) -- the defining balance between folk intimacy and chamber sophistication.
Dynamic range: E=2-3 quiet passages (solo guitar/voice) to E=4-5 crescendo peaks (full chamber ensemble). Average E=4.
theory_nodes.description: The chamber quiet: restrained dynamics within a rich timbral palette. E=4 balance point.

ancestors: The supergroup orbit (degree 123, Wellwater Conspiracy) -- E=6. The chamber quiet at E=4 is the post-maximum moderation.
Chamber folk lineage: Damien Jurado (45), Sera Cahoone (48), Laura Gibson (57).
genealogy.relationship: Acoustic element at degree 124. Track at degree 125.

theory_nodes.concept: Kill Rock Stars enters the engine -- landmark label entry: founded Olympia 1991 by Slim Moon; Bikini Kill, Sleater-Kinney, Elliott Smith, Unwound, The Decemberists; independence as aesthetic and ethical commitment; riot grrrl distribution channel
examples: Horse Feathers (degree 124) -- first direct KRS entry in the engine.
KRS roster: Bikini Kill (riot grrrl), Sleater-Kinney (post-punk), Elliott Smith (singer-songwriter), Unwound (post-hardcore), Decemberists (literary indie rock).
Olympia on I-5 between Portland and Seattle -- the label as geographic hub.
theory_nodes.description: Kill Rock Stars: first direct entry. The legendary Olympia label. Independence as principle.

ancestors: Hollywood Records (degree 123) -- Disney-owned label. Corporate vs. independent in consecutive degrees.
Sub Pop label entries throughout the engine. KRS is the independent-label counterweight.
genealogy.relationship: KRS label entry at degree 124. Track label appearances going forward.

theory_nodes.concept: Portland-Seattle corridor -- cultural pattern: one scene with two centers of gravity; Portland (literary folk, DIY) plus Seattle (label infrastructure, visibility) plus Olympia (KRS, the hub); I-5 as cultural artery
examples: Horse Feathers: Portland origins, Olympia label (KRS), Seattle distribution and visibility.
I-5 between Portland and Seattle: three hours. Musicians move between constantly.
Olympia sits between: K Records, Kill Rock Stars, riot grrrl. The geographic hub.
theory_nodes.description: The Portland-Seattle corridor: one scene, two centers of gravity, Olympia as hub.

ancestors: Portland artists in the engine. Seattle artists in the engine. The corridor is the connection.
genealogy.relationship: Corridor pattern at degree 124. Track corridor connections.

cross_references.relationship: SAME PROJECT. Horse Feathers appeared at degree 70. Degree 124 is the KRS-focused entry documenting the label connection and chamber experimental evolution.
ENERGY DROP. E=6 (Wellwater, Hollywood Records) to E=4 (Horse Feathers, Kill Rock Stars). Maximum to moderate in one degree. Disney-owned to independent. The single-spike return.
CHAMBER FOLK LINEAGE. Both quiet, introspective singer-songwriters with chamber arrangements. Both on independent labels. Both in the literary folk tradition of the PNW.
INDIE LINEAGE. Both indie projects from PNW independent label ecosystem. Both quiet-foundation artists who build to dynamic peaks.
LABEL CONTRAST. No-Anchor (self-released, E=6, noise/experimental) vs. Horse Feathers (Kill Rock Stars, E=4, chamber folk). Two poles of PNW independent music.
